comments.body: STATUS (session handoff): P1 (1f3d) LANDED a6cdad0e2 + CLOSED. P2 (4b59) LANDED e6f3283a0, close pending follow-up 778 (unification). Test-hardening folded into P1/P2 (absolute-direction tests vs captured live-Jira fixtures). NEW children filed: observability 48c8-5375-f883-462d, Jira cleanup bd80-0603-7d44-4d9f (both pending, see their descriptions). Also landed OUT OF EPIC: actionlint CI-flake fix 4e99 (98e95ab24, closed). Sequencing: cleanup after P1 reconciler-deploy; observability activation after cleanup.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: SESSION-2 PROGRESS: (1) Gerrit 778 SUBMITTED/MERGED (origin/main af433d2b4) — link-direction unification; P2 (4b59) CLOSED via completion-verifier against the landed code. (2) Observability (48c8, CLAIMED) implemented + RED→GREEN tested + landed as Gerrit change 785 (fix/reconciler-observability): apply_handlers.py promotes swallowed PARTIAL comment drops to outcome[error] behind RECONCILER_FAIL_SILENT_NOOP (create+update; canary augments not clobbers); new tests isolate the partial-drop gap + add comments/labels canary coverage. Dropped the optional reconcile.py telemetry (would have breached reconcile.py's 1093 module-size ceiling for an over-cap file). Full suite green modulo pre-existing local-env flakes (config_show/perf + 5 identity_gate + 3 REPO_ROOT-leak — all confirmed pre-existing on clean tree). 785 CI running (~58min whole-store verify-identity gate). Flag NOT activated (gated on bd80 cleanup). (3) bd80 plan remediated 5 rounds (8→2→1→1→1 blocking); all SUBSTANTIVE safety findings fixed (ADF-aware marker detection, reuse of _normalize_comment_body, paginated fetch+total reconciliation, single 429 route via _call_with_retry, rollback, quiescing). Plan-review now in a self-referential infinite regress (each added detail spawns a 'no AC for that detail' block). Cleanup EXECUTION is human-gated (irreversible) regardless.

compiled_state.description:
Jira reconciler silently fails to sync comments and inbound links while reporting
`converged` — three distinct defects, all hidden behind a pass that counts failed
sub-operations toward "applied N of N" and exits 0.

## Context

Investigation (/rebar-debug, 2026-07-17) established that status/existence sync is
healthy (0 drift across ~1200 tickets), but three field-level sync paths are broken
and invisible:

- **Comment sync (P1):** `get_comments()` returns only the first 50 of N comments
  (no `--paginate`), so outbound dedup re-posts the rest every pass. 13 tickets have
  been inflated to Jira's 5000-comment hard cap (local truth: 22-76 comments each);
  further adds fail `COMMENTS_PER_ISSUE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` and are swallowed.
- **Inbound link direction (P2):** `_diff_links_inbound` (inbound_differ.py:519-527)
  AND `_diff_link_removals` (outbound_links.py:198-203) both invert the
  inward/outward -> blocks/depends_on mapping. Latent since 2026-06-15 (`f6687adf4`);
  UNMASKED when the outbound direction fix landed (`6b25dbead`/`43d154523`, 07-16/17)
  — the two inversions previously cancelled on round-trip. Now ~815 inbound link
  applies/pass are cycle-rejected by rebar's guard.
- **Observability (meta):** a pass with failed sub-ops still prints `applied N of N`
  / exits 0 / logs `converged`. The `RECONCILER_FAIL_SILENT_NOOP` flag (default off)
  exists but is not enabled; swallowed comment failures land in `comment_errors` and
  are never counted as failures.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] P1: outbound comment sync is idempotent — a steady-state pass emits 0 comment
      adds for a ticket already mirrored, even when the Jira side has >50 comments.
- [ ] P2: an inbound Jira `Blocks` link round-trips to the correct rebar relation
      (`blocks`/`depends_on`) with NO cycle-rejection against the correct local edge,
      in BOTH the add path and the removal path.
- [ ] Observability: a pass with any silent sub-op no-op reports failure (non-zero
      exit) rather than `converged`, once P1/P2 remove the standing noise.
- [ ] Test hardening: unit + integration + E2E coverage added for all three failure
      modes AND same-kind gaps; the new tests fail on the pre-fix code.
- [ ] Jira data cleanup: the 13 capped tickets are de-duplicated so new comments can
      sync again — executed AFTER P1 deploys to main, under the safety plan below.

## Jira cleanup safety plan (T4 — destructive-step rollback)

The comment de-duplication is IRREVERSIBLE (Jira comment deletion cannot be undone
via API). It is a deliberate, justified destructive step (the tickets are permanently
unable to sync until duplicates are purged), executed only with these guardrails:

- [ ] **Pre-deletion backup:** export the FULL comment payload (id, body, author,
      created) for all 13 tickets to a versioned JSON artifact BEFORE any deletion, so
      any comment can be re-created if needed.
- [ ] **Dry-run first:** the cleanup script has a `--dry-run` (default) mode that
      lists exactly which comment IDs would be deleted and which one canonical copy of
      each distinct body is kept; a human reviews the dry-run diff before `--execute`.
- [ ] **Keep-one dedup rule:** for each distinct normalized body, keep the OLDEST
      copy (lowest created) and delete the rest; never delete a body with only one copy.
      Only delete comments authored by the bridge bot identity (never human comments).
- [ ] **Idempotent + resumable:** re-running is safe; on partial failure, re-run
      `--dry-run` to see the remaining duplicates and resume. Deletions are logged
      (ticket, comment id, body hash) to an artifact as they happen.
- [ ] **Sequencing:** run ONLY after P1 is deployed to main, else the buggy
      reconciler re-inflates duplicates within minutes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All child tickets (P1, P2, observability, test-hardening, Jira-cleanup) are closed.
- [ ] A reconcile pass shows 0 comment-cap failures and 0 inbound link
      cycle-rejections for the previously-affected tickets.
- [ ] The cleanup backup artifact + dry-run log exist before any live deletion.
